  1. Kalman-and-Bayesian-Filters-in-Python
  2. 04-One-Dimensional-Kalman-Filters.ipynb

    3. Python 中的卡尔曼滤波与贝叶斯滤波 04：一维卡尔曼滤波器.ipynb
Notebook

Table of Contents  目录

One Dimensional Kalman Filters
一维卡尔曼滤波器¶

In [1]:  在[1]中：
%matplotlib inline
In [2]:  在[2]中：
#format the book
import book_format
book_format.set_style()
Out[2]:  Out[2]：

Now that we understand the discrete Bayes filter and Gaussians we are prepared to implement a Kalman filter. We will do this exactly as we did the discrete Bayes filter - rather than starting with equations we will develop the code step by step based on reasoning about the problem.
既然我们已经了解了离散贝叶斯滤波器和高斯分布，那么现在就可以开始实现卡尔曼滤波器了。我们的实现方式将与处理离散贝叶斯滤波器时完全相同：不会从方程式开始，而是通过逐步分析问题来编写代码。

"One dimensional" means that the filter only tracks one state variable, such as position on the x-axis. In subsequent chapters we will learn a more general multidimensional form of the filter that can track many state variables simultaneously, such as position, velocity, and acceleration. Recall that we used velocity in the g-h filter to get better estimates than by tracking position alone. The same is true for the Kalman filter.
“一维”的意思是指，该滤波器只跟踪一个状态变量，比如 x 轴上的位置。在后面的章节中，我们将学习更通用的一维滤波器形式，这种滤波器能够同时跟踪多个状态变量，比如位置、速度和加速度。记得在 g-h 滤波器中，我们利用了速度这一参数，从而得到了比仅跟踪位置更准确的估计结果。卡尔曼滤波器也是如此。

So why not just jump into the multidimensional form of the filter? To be honest, the math is difficult, and my intuitive approach to developing the filter starts to break down. This math obscures the rather simple principles that allow the Kalman filter to work.
那么，为什么不直接采用多维形式的滤波器呢？说实话，相关的数学计算相当复杂，我试图用直观的方法来设计滤波器，但这种方法已经不再适用了。这些复杂的数学公式掩盖了卡尔曼滤波器运作所依据的那些简单原理。

So, in this chapter we learn how to use Gaussians to implement a Bayesian filter. That's all the Kalman filter is - a Bayesian filter that uses Gaussians. In the next chapter we will switch to a multidimensional form and the full power of the Kalman filter will be unleashed!
在这一章中，我们学习了如何利用高斯分布来构建贝叶斯滤波器。实际上，卡尔曼滤波器本质上就是一种利用高斯分布的贝叶斯滤波器。在下一章中，我们将把模型扩展到多维形式，届时卡尔曼滤波器的全部优势就能得以充分发挥了！

Problem Description  问题描述¶

As in the Discrete Bayes Filter chapter we will be tracking a moving object in a long hallway at work. Assume that in our latest hackathon someone created an RFID tracker that provides a reasonably accurate position of the dog. The sensor returns the distance of the dog from the left end of the hallway in meters. So, 23.4 would mean the dog is 23.4 meters from the left end of the hallway.
与在“离散贝叶斯滤波器”那一章中类似，我们在这里也要追踪工作场所里走廊中移动的物体。假设在最近的黑客马拉松活动中，有人开发出了一个 RFID 追踪器，能够相当准确地确定狗的位置。该传感器会反馈狗距离走廊左端的距离，单位为米。因此，23.4 米的数值意味着狗距离走廊左端 23.4 米远。

The sensor is not perfect. A reading of 23.4 could correspond to the dog being at 23.7, or 23.0. However, it is very unlikely to correspond to a position of 47.6. Testing during the hackathon confirmed this result - the sensor is 'reasonably' accurate, and while it had errors, the errors are small. Furthermore, the errors seemed to be evenly distributed on both sides of the true position; a position of 23 m would equally likely be measured as 22.9 or 23.1. Perhaps we can model this with a Gaussian.
这种传感器并不完美。例如，当测量值为 23.4 时，实际上狗可能位于 23.7 或 23.0 的位置。不过，测量值为 47.6 的可能性则极低。在黑客松期间的测试也证实了这一结果：该传感器的精度“相当不错”。虽然存在一定的误差，但这些误差都很小。此外，这些误差似乎在真实位置的上下范围内均匀分布；也就是说，距离为 23 米的物体，其测量值很可能是 22.9 或 23.1。或许我们可以用高斯函数来描述这种误差分布。

We predict that the dog is moving. This prediction is not perfect. Sometimes our prediction will overshoot, sometimes it will undershoot. We are more likely to undershoot or overshoot by a little than a lot. Perhaps we can also model this with a Gaussian.
我们预测这只狗正在移动。不过，这种预测并不完美。有时候，我们的预测会偏高；有时候则偏低。通常情况下，预测值会稍微偏高或偏低一点，而不会偏差太大。或许，我们可以用高斯函数来模拟这一现象。

Beliefs as Gaussians  将信念视为高斯分布¶

We can express our belief in the dog's position with a Gaussian. Say we believe that our dog is at 10 meters, and the variance in that belief is 1 m22, or N(10,1)N(10,1). A plot of the pdf follows:
我们可以用高斯函数来表示我们对狗狗所在位置的判断。假设我们认为狗狗位于 10 米处，那么这种判断的方差为 1 米。相应的概率密度函数如下图所示：

In [3]:  在[3]中：
import filterpy.stats as stats
stats.plot_gaussian_pdf(mean=10., variance=1., 
                        xlim=(4, 16), ylim=(0, .5));

This plot depicts our uncertainty about the dog's position. It represents a fairly inexact belief. While we believe that it is most likely that the dog is at 10 m, any position from 9 m to 11 m or so are quite likely as well. Assume the dog is standing still, and we query the sensor again. This time it returns 10.2 m. Can we use this additional information to improve our estimate?
该图表反映了我们对狗所在位置的不确定性。这其实是一种相当不精确的估计。虽然我们认为狗很可能位于 10 米处，但 9 米到 11 米之间的任何位置也都有可能。假设狗静止不动，我们再次询问传感器。这次，传感器显示的距离是 10.2 米。那么，我们能否利用这一额外信息来更准确地估计狗的位置呢？

Intuition suggests we can. Consider: if we read the sensor 500 times and each time it returned a value between 8 and 12, all centered around 10, we should be very confident that the dog is near 10. Of course, a different interpretation is possible. Perhaps our dog was randomly wandering back and forth in a way that exactly emulated random draws from a normal distribution. But that seems extremely unlikely - I've never seen a dog do that. Let's look at 500 draws from N(10,1)N(10,1):
直觉告诉我们，我们是可以这么做的。试想一下：如果我们读取传感器的数值 500 次，而每次得到的数值都在 8 到 12 之间，且数值都接近 10，那么我们就有充分的理由相信，那只狗确实就在 10 附近。当然，也有可能有其他解释。也许那只狗只是随机地来回移动，其运动轨迹恰好符合正态分布的随机抽样规律。但这种可能性极低——我从未见过狗会有这样的行为。让我们来看看 N(10,1)N(10,1) 的 500 次测量结果吧：

In [4]:  在[4]中：
import numpy as np
from numpy.random import randn
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

xs = range(500)
ys = randn(500)*1. + 10.
plt.plot(xs, ys)
print(f'Mean of readings is {np.mean(ys):.3f}')

Mean of readings is 9.936

Eyeballing this confirms our intuition - no dog moves like this. However, noisy sensor data certainly looks this way. The computed mean of the readings is almost exactly 10. Assuming the dog is standing still, we say the dog is at position 10 with a variance of 1.
凭肉眼观察，这一结果与我们的直觉相符——没有狗会以这种方式移动。不过，传感器检测到的数据确实呈现如此形态。各项测量值的算术平均值恰好为 10。假设狗处于静止状态，那么可以说狗位于位置 10，其误差范围为 1。

Tracking with Gaussian Probabilities
基于高斯概率的跟踪算法¶

The discrete Bayes filter used a histogram of probabilities to track the dog. Each bin in the histogram represents a position, and the value is the probability of the dog being in that position.
这种离散贝叶斯滤波器利用概率直方图来追踪狗的位置。直方图中的每个区间代表一个位置，而该区间的数值则代表了狗处于该位置的概率。

Tracking was performed with a cycle of predictions and updates. We used the equations
跟踪过程是通过一系列的预测和更新来实现的。我们使用了相应的数学公式来进行计算。

x¯x=x∗fx(∙)=L⋅x¯PredictUpdatex¯=x∗fx(∙)Predictx=L⋅x¯Update

to compute the new probability distributions. Recall that x¯x¯ is the prior, LL is the likelihood of a measurement given the prior x¯x¯, fx(∙)fx(∙) is the process model, and ∗∗ denotes convolution. xx is bold to denote that it is a histogram of numbers, or a vector.
为了计算新的概率分布，需要记住： x¯x¯ 代表先验概率； LL 表示在给定先验概率 x¯x¯ 的条件下，某次测量的概率； fx(∙)fx(∙) 代表过程模型； ∗∗ 表示卷积操作。 xx 以粗体标出，表示其实际上是一个数值的直方图或向量。

This method works, but led to histograms that implied the dog could be in multiple places at once. Also, the computations are very slow for large problems.
这种方法确实有效，但生成的直方图表明，那只狗可能同时出现在多个位置上。此外，对于规模较大的问题，这种计算方式的速度极其缓慢。

Can we replace xx, the histogram, with a Gaussian N(x,σ2)N(x,σ2)? Absolutely! We've learned how to express belief as a Gaussian. A Gaussian, which is a single number pair N(μ,σ2),N(μ,σ2), can replace an entire histogram of probabilities:
我们能把直方图 xx 替换为高斯分布 N(x,σ2)N(x,σ2) 吗？当然可以！我们已经知道如何用高斯分布来表示概率。高斯分布其实只是一个数字对 N(μ,σ2),N(μ,σ2), ，它完全可以替代整个概率直方图。

In [5]:  在[5]中：
import kf_book.kf_internal as kf_internal
kf_internal.gaussian_vs_histogram()

I hope you see the power of this. We can replace hundreds to thousands of numbers with a single pair of numbers: x=N(μ,σ2)x=N(μ,σ2).
希望你能明白其中的妙处。我们可以用一对数字 x=N(μ,σ2)x=N(μ,σ2) 来替代成百上千个数字。

The tails of the Gaussian extend to infinity on both sides, so it incorporates arbitrarily many bars in the histogram. If this represents our belief in the position of the dog in the hallway, this one Gaussian covers the entire hallway (and the entire universe on that axis). We think that it is likely the dog is at 10, but he could be at 8, 14, or, with infinitesimally small probability, at 108080.
高斯曲线的尾部向两侧无限延伸，因此该曲线能够涵盖直方图中的任意多个数值。如果用这种方式来表示我们对狗在走廊上位置的判断，那么这个高斯曲线就涵盖了整个走廊（以及该轴线上的整个“宇宙”）。我们认为狗很可能位于 10 号位置，但也有可能位于 8 号或 14 号位置；当然，狗位于 10 号位置的概率也是极小的。

In this chapter we replace histograms with Gaussians:
在本章中，我们用高斯分布来替代直方图：

discrete Bayesx¯=x∗f(x)x=∥Lx¯∥Gaussianx¯N=xN⊕fxN(∙)xN=L⊗x¯NStepPredictUpdatediscrete BayesGaussianStepx¯=x∗f(x)x¯N=xN⊕fxN(∙)Predictx=‖Lx¯‖xN=L⊗x¯NUpdate

where ⊕⊕ and ⊗⊗ is meant to express some unknown operator on Gaussians. I won't do it in the rest of the book, but the subscript indicates that xNxN is a Gaussian.
其中， ⊕⊕ 和 ⊗⊗ 表示对高斯函数施加的某种未知运算。在本书的其余部分，我不会再使用这种表示方法。不过，下标表明 xNxN 确实是一个高斯函数。

The discrete Bayes filter used convolution for the prediction. We showed that it used the total probabability theorem, computed as a sum, so maybe we can add the Gaussians. It used multiplications to incorporate the measurement into the prior, so maybe we can multiply the Gaussians. Could it be this easy:
这种离散贝叶斯滤波器在预测过程中使用了卷积运算。我们发现，该滤波器实际上运用了全概率定理，即将各种概率相加来得到最终结果。因此，或许我们可以将高斯函数也相加起来。此外，该滤波器还通过乘法运算将测量结果纳入先验概率中，所以也许我们也可以对高斯函数进行乘法运算。难道真的有那么简单吗？

x¯x=?x+fx(∙)=?L⋅x¯x¯=?x+fx(∙)x=?L⋅x¯

This will only work if the sum and product of two Gaussians is another Gaussian. Otherwise after the first epoch xx would not be Gaussian, and this scheme falls apart.
只有当两个高斯函数的和与积仍然是高斯函数时，这种算法才有效。否则，在第一个训练周期之后， xx 就不再满足高斯函数的性质，整个算法也就无法继续运行了。

Predictions with Gaussians
高斯模型预测结果¶

We use Newton's equation of motion to compute current position based on the current velocity and previous position:
我们利用牛顿运动定律，根据当前速度和先前的位置来计算出当前位置。

x¯k=xk−1+vkΔt=xk−1+fxx¯k=xk−1+vkΔt=xk−1+fx

I've dropped the notation fx(∙)fx(∙) in favor of fxfx to keep the equations uncluttered.
为了使方程更简洁明了，我不再使用 fx(∙)fx(∙) 这个符号，而改用 fxfx 。

If the dog is at 10 m, his velocity is 15 m/s, and the epoch is 2 seconds long, we have
如果狗位于 10 米处，其速度为 15 米/秒，且时间间隔为 2 秒，那么我们可以得到：

fxx¯k=vΔt=15⋅2=10+(15⋅2)=40fx=vΔt=15⋅2x¯k=10+(15⋅2)=40

We are uncertain about his current position and velocity, so this will not do. We need to express the uncertainty with a Gaussian.
我们无法确定他目前的所在位置和速度，因此这种表示方式不可行。我们需要用高斯函数来表示这种不确定性。

Position is easy. We define xx as a Gaussian. If we think the dog is at 10 m, and the standard deviation of our uncertainty is 0.2 m, we get x=N(10,0.22)x=N(10,0.22).
定位其实很简单。我们将 xx 定义为高斯分布。如果我们认为狗位于 10 米处，而我们的不确定性标准差为 0.2 米，那么就能得出 x=N(10,0.22)x=N(10,0.22) 的值。

What about our uncertainty in his movement? We define fxfx as a Gaussian. If the dog's velocity is 15 m/s, the epoch is 1 second, and the standard deviation of our uncertainty is 0.7 m/s, we get fx=N(15,0.72)fx=N(15,0.72).
那我们对他移动路径的不确定性又该如何处理呢？我们将这种不确定性定义为高斯分布。如果狗的速度为 15 米/秒，时间间隔为 1 秒，而不确定性的标准差为 0.7 米/秒，那么我们就能计算出 fx=N(15,0.72)fx=N(15,0.72) 的值了。

The equation for the prior is
先验概率的公式为：

x¯=x+fxx¯=x+fx

What is the sum of two Gaussians? In the last chapter I proved that:
两个高斯函数的和是多少呢？在上一章中，我已经证明了：

μ=μ1+μ2σ2=σ21+σ22μ=μ1+μ2σ2=σ12+σ22

This is fantastic news; the sum of two Gaussians is another Gaussian!
这真是个好消息：两个高斯函数的和仍然是一个高斯函数！

The math works, but does this make intuitive sense? Think of the physical representation of this abstract equation. We have
从数学上来说，这个公式是成立的。但是，从直观上来看，这真的合理吗？试着想象一下这个抽象方程在现实世界中的体现吧。我们有……

x=N(10,0.22)fx=N(15,0.72)x=N(10,0.22)fx=N(15,0.72)

If we add these we get:
如果把它们加起来，就得到：

x¯σ¯2=μx+μfx=10+15=σ2x+σ2fx=0.22+0.72=25=0.53x¯=μx+μfx=10+15=25σ¯2=σx2+σfx2=0.22+0.72=0.53

It makes sense that the predicted position is the previous position plus the movement. What about the variance? It is harder to form an intuition about this. However, recall that with the predict() function for the discrete Bayes filter we always lost information. We don't really know where the dog is moving, so the confidence should get smaller (variance gets larger). σ2fxσfx2 is the amount of uncertainty added to the system due to the imperfect prediction about the movement, and so we would add that to the existing uncertainty.
预测位置等于上一个位置加上移动距离，这很合乎逻辑。那么，方差呢？这一点较难直观理解。不过，请回想一下：在使用离散贝叶斯滤波器的 predict() 函数时，我们总会丢失一些信息。我们其实并不知道狗到底移动到了哪里，因此置信度应该会降低（方差则会上升）。 σ2fxσfx2 代表了由于对狗的移动方向预测不准确而导致的不确定性。所以，我们需要把这一不确定性加到现有的不确定性上。

Let's take advantage of the namedtuple class in Python's collection module to implement a Gaussian object. We could implement a Gaussian using a tuple, where N(10,0.04)N(10,0.04) is implemented in Python as g = (10., 0.04). We would access the mean with g[0] and the variance with g[1].
我们可以利用 Python 的 collection 模块中的 namedtuple 类来创建高斯对象。其实，我们也可以用元组来表示高斯分布，因为在 Python 中， N(10,0.04)N(10,0.04) 实际上就是以 g = (10., 0.04) 的形式实现的。我们可以通过 g[0] 来获取高斯分布的均值，通过 g[1] 来获取方差。

namedtuple works the same as a tuple, except you provide it with a type name and field names. It's not important to understand, but I modified the __repr__ method to display its value using the notation in this chapter.
namedtuple 的用法与元组类似，只不过需要指定数据的类型和各个字段的名称。虽然理解这一点并非必要，但我修改了 __repr__ 方法，使其能按照本章所介绍的格式来显示其值。

In [6]:  在[6]中：
from collections import namedtuple
gaussian = namedtuple('Gaussian', ['mean', 'var'])
gaussian.__repr__ = lambda s: f'𝒩(μ={s[0]:.3f}, 𝜎²={s[1]:.3f})'

Now we can create a print a Gaussian with:
现在，我们可以用以下方式来生成高斯分布的图像了：

In [7]:  在[7]中：
g1 = gaussian(3.4, 10.1)
g2 = gaussian(mean=4.5, var=0.2**2)
print(g1)
print(g2)

𝒩(μ=3.400, 𝜎²=10.100)
𝒩(μ=4.500, 𝜎²=0.040)

We can access the mean and variance with either subscripts or field names:
我们可以通过下标或字段名称来获取均值和方差：

In [8]:  在[8]中：
g1.mean, g1[0], g1[1], g1.var
Out[8]:  输出[8]：
(3.4, 3.4, 10.1, 10.1)

Here is our implementation of the predict function, where pos and movement are Gaussian tuples in the form (μμ, σ2σ2):
以下是我们对“predict”函数的实现方式。其中， pos 和 movement 都是高斯元组，其形式为( μμ , σ2σ2 )。

In [9]:  在[9]中：
def predict(pos, movement):
    return gaussian(pos.mean + movement.mean, pos.var + movement.var)

Let's test it. What is the prior if the intitial position is the Gaussian N(10,0.22)N(10,0.22) and the movement is the Gaussian N(15,0.72)N(15,0.72)?
我们来测试一下。如果初始位置服从高斯分布 N(10,0.22)N(10,0.22) ，而运动过程也服从高斯分布 N(15,0.72)N(15,0.72) ，那么先验概率是多少呢？

In [10]:  在[10]中：
pos = gaussian(10., .2**2)
move = gaussian(15., .7**2)
predict(pos, move)
Out[10]:  Out[10]：
𝒩(μ=25.000, 𝜎²=0.530)

The prior states that the dog is at 25 m with a variance of 0.53 m22, which is what we computed by hand.
先前的结果显示，那只狗的距离为 25 米，误差为 0.53 米。这一结果与我们手动计算的结果一致。

Updates with Gaussians  高斯函数的更新情况¶

The discrete Bayes filter encodes our belief about the position of our dog in a histogram of probabilities. The distribution is discrete and multimodal. It can express strong belief that the dog is in two positions at once, and the positions are discrete.
离散贝叶斯滤波器将我们对狗所在位置的判断以概率直方图的形式表现出来。这种概率分布是离散的、多模态的。它意味着我们可以同时认为狗处于两个不同的位置上，而这些位置都是离散的。

We are proposing that we replace the histogram with a Gaussian. The discrete Bayes filter used this code to compute the posterior:
我们建议用高斯分布来替代直方图。所使用的离散贝叶斯滤波器正是利用这一公式来计算后验概率的。

def update(likelihood, prior):
    posterior = likelihood * prior
    return normalize(posterior)

which is an implementation of the equation:
这是该方程式的具体实现方式：

x=∥Lx¯∥x=‖Lx¯‖

We've just shown that we can represent the prior with a Gaussian. What about the likelihood? The likelihood is the probability of the measurement given the current state. We've learned how to represent measurements as Gaussians. For example, maybe our sensor states that the dog is at 23 m, with a standard deviation of 0.4 meters. Our measurement, expressed as a likelihood, is z=N(23,0.16)z=N(23,0.16).
我们刚刚证明了可以用高斯分布来表示先验概率。那么，似然概率呢？似然概率指的是在当前状态下，进行某次测量的概率。我们已经知道如何用高斯分布来表示各种测量结果。例如，假设我们的传感器检测到狗位于 23 米处，标准差为 0.4 米。那么，用似然概率来表示的话，就是 z=N(23,0.16)z=N(23,0.16) 。

Both the likelihood and prior are modeled with Gaussians. Can we multiply Gaussians? Is the product of two Gaussians another Gaussian?
概率分布和先验概率都用高斯函数来建模。那么，高斯函数可以相乘吗？两个高斯函数的乘积还是高斯函数吗？

Yes to the former, and almost to the latter! In the last chapter I proved that the product of two Gaussians is proportional to another Gausian.
前者当然成立，后者也几乎成立！在上一章中，我已经证明：两个高斯函数的乘积与另一个高斯函数成正比。

μσ2=σ21μ2+σ22μ1σ21+σ22,=σ21σ22σ21+σ22μ=σ12μ2+σ22μ1σ12+σ22,σ2=σ12σ22σ12+σ22

We can immediately infer several things. If we normalize the result, the product is another Gaussian. If one Gaussian is the likelihood, and the second is the prior, then the mean is a scaled sum of the prior and the measurement. The variance is a combination of the variances of the prior and measurement. Finally, the variances are completely unaffected by the values of the mean!
我们可以立即得出几个结论。如果对结果进行标准化处理，那么乘积仍然是一个高斯分布。如果其中一个高斯分布代表“似然值”，另一个代表“先验概率”，那么平均值就是先验概率与测量结果的加权之和。方差则是先验概率的方差与测量结果的方差的组合。最后，方差的值完全不受平均值的影响！

We put this in Bayesian terms like so:
我们可以用贝叶斯术语来表达这一点：

N(μ,σ2)=∥prior⋅likelihood∥=∥N(μ¯,σ¯2)⋅N(μz,σ2z)∥=N(σ¯2μz+σ2zμ¯σ¯2+σ2z,σ¯2σ2zσ¯2+σ2z)N(μ,σ2)=‖prior⋅likelihood‖=‖N(μ¯,σ¯2)⋅N(μz,σz2)‖=N(σ¯2μz+σz2μ¯σ¯2+σz2,σ¯2σz2σ¯2+σz2)

If we implemented that in a function gaussian_multiply() we could implement our filter's update step as
如果我们在函数 gaussian_multiply() 中实现这一点，那么就可以将过滤器的更新步骤也一并实现出来。

In [11]:  在[11]中：
def gaussian_multiply(g1, g2):
    mean = (g1.var * g2.mean + g2.var * g1.mean) / (g1.var + g2.var)
    variance = (g1.var * g2.var) / (g1.var + g2.var)
    return gaussian(mean, variance)

def update(prior, likelihood):
    posterior = gaussian_multiply(likelihood, prior)
    return posterior

# test the update function
predicted_pos = gaussian(10., .2**2)
measured_pos = gaussian(11., .1**2)
estimated_pos = update(predicted_pos, measured_pos)
estimated_pos
Out[11]:  输出[11]：
𝒩(μ=10.800, 𝜎²=0.008)

Perhaps this would be clearer if we used more specific names:
如果我们使用更具体的名称来表述，或许会更容易理解：

def update_dog(dog_pos, measurement):
    estimated_pos = gaussian_multiply(measurement, dog_pos)
    return estimated_pos

That is less abstract, which perhaps helps with comprehension, but it is poor coding practice. We are writing a Kalman filter that works for any problem, not just tracking dogs in a hallway, so we won't use variable names with 'dog' in them. Also, this form obscures the fact that we are multiplying the likelihood by the prior.
这样虽然不那么抽象了，或许有助于理解，但这并不是良好的编程习惯。我们正在编写的卡尔曼滤波器应该适用于各种问题，而不仅仅是用于跟踪走廊里的狗。因此，我们不会使用包含“dog”这个词的变量名。此外，这种写法也让人看不清：实际上，我们是在将概率与先验概率相乘而已。

We have the majority of our filter implemented, but I fear this step is still a bit confusing. I've asserted that we can multiply Gaussians and that it correctly performs the update step, but why is this true? Let's take a detour and spend some time multiplying Gaussians.
我们已经实现了大部分的过滤功能，但我觉得这一步还是有点复杂。我确认过，高斯函数是可以相乘的，而且这种操作确实能够正确地完成更新步骤。但为什么会是这样呢？让我们花点时间来仔细研究一下高斯函数的相乘运算吧。

Understanding Gaussian Multiplication
理解高斯乘法运算¶

Let's plot the pdf of N(10,1)×N(10,1)N(10,1)×N(10,1). Can you determine its shape without looking at the result? What should the new mean be? Will the curve be wider, narrower, or the same as N(10,1)N(10,1)?
让我们来绘制 N(10,1)×N(10,1)N(10,1)×N(10,1) 的概率密度函数吧。不用查看结果，你能判断出它的形状吗？新的均值应该是多少呢？这条曲线会比 N(10,1)N(10,1) 的更宽、更窄，还是保持不变呢？

In [12]:  在[12]中：
z = gaussian(10., 1.)  # Gaussian N(10, 1)

product = gaussian_multiply(z, z)

xs = np.arange(5, 15, 0.1)
ys = [stats.gaussian(x, z.mean, z.var) for x in xs]
plt.plot(xs, ys, label='$\mathcal{N}(10,1)$')

ys = [stats.gaussian(x, product.mean, product.var) for x in xs]
plt.plot(xs, ys, label='$\mathcal{N}(10,1) \\times \mathcal{N}(10,1)$', ls='--')
plt.legend()
print(product)

𝒩(μ=10.000, 𝜎²=0.500)

The result of the multiplication is taller and narrow than the original Gaussian but the mean is unchanged. Does this match your intuition?
乘法运算后的结果比原始的高斯分布更细长，但均值保持不变。这一结果符合你的直觉吗？

Think of the Gaussians as two measurements. If I measure twice and get 10 meters each time, I should conclude that the length is close to 10 meters. Thus the mean should be 10. It would make no sense to conclude the length is actually 11, or 9.5. Also, I am more confident with two measurements than with one, so the variance of the result should be smaller.
可以把高斯分布看作是两次测量结果。如果我进行了两次测量，每次得到的数值都是 10 米，那么我应该认为该长度接近 10 米。因此，平均值应该是 10 米。认为该长度实际上是 11 米或 9.5 米是没有道理的。此外，两次测量的结果比一次测量的结果更可靠，所以测量结果的方差应该更小。

"Measure twice, cut once" is a well known saying. Gaussian multiplication is a mathematical model of this physical fact.
“三思而后行”是一句众所周知的格言。高斯乘法正是这一物理现象的数学体现。

I'm unlikely to get the same measurement twice in a row. Now let's plot the pdf of N(10.2,1)×N(9.7,1)N(10.2,1)×N(9.7,1). What do you think the result will be? Think about it, and then look at the graph.
我不太可能连续两次得到相同的测量结果。现在，让我们来绘制 N(10.2,1)×N(9.7,1)N(10.2,1)×N(9.7,1) 的概率密度函数吧。你认为结果会怎样呢？先好好思考一下，然后再看看图表吧。

In [13]:  在[13]中：
m = 3.3
f'$({m},{m})$'
Out[13]:  输出[13]：
'$(3.3,3.3)$'
In [14]:  在[14]中：
def plot_products(g1, g2): 
    plt.figure()
    product = gaussian_multiply(g1, g2)

    xs = np.arange(5, 15, 0.1)
    ys = [stats.gaussian(x, g1.mean, g1.var) for x in xs]
    plt.plot(xs, ys, label='$\mathcal{N}$' + f'$({g1.mean},{g1.var})$')

    ys = [stats.gaussian(x, g2.mean, g2.var) for x in xs]
    plt.plot(xs, ys, label='$\mathcal{N}$' + '$({g2.mean},{ge.var})$')

    ys = [stats.gaussian(x, product.mean, product.var) for x in xs]
    plt.plot(xs, ys, label='product', ls='--')
    plt.legend();
    plt.show()
    
z1 = gaussian(10.2, 1)
z2 = gaussian(9.7, 1)
 
plot_products(z1, z2)

If you ask two people to measure the distance of a table from a wall, and one gets 10.2 meters, and the other got 9.7 meters, your best guess must be the average, 9.95 meters if you trust the skills of both equally.
如果你让两个人来测量桌子到墙的距离，其中一个人测得 10.2 米，另一个人测得 9.7 米，那么如果你对两人的测量能力都相当信任的话，最合理的估计应该是两者的平均值，即 9.95 米。

Recall the g-h filter. We agreed that if I weighed myself on two scales, and the first read 160 lbs while the second read 170 lbs, and both were equally accurate, the best estimate was 165 lbs. Furthermore I should be a bit more confident about 165 lbs vs 160 lbs or 170 lbs because I now have two readings, both near this estimate, increasing my confidence that neither is wildly wrong.
请回想一下 g-h 滤波器的相关原理。我们之前说过，如果我用两台秤来称体重，第一台秤显示 160 磅，第二台秤显示 170 磅，而这两台秤的测量精度都相当准确的话，那么最合理的估计值应该是 165 磅。此外，与 160 磅或 170 磅相比，我更应该相信 165 磅这个数值。因为现在我有两个接近 165 磅的测量结果，这就增加了我对这个数值的信任度，因为可以排除其中某个数值出现严重误差的可能性。

This becomes counter-intuitive in more complicated situations, so let's consider it further. Perhaps a more reasonable assumption would be that one person made a mistake, and the true distance is either 10.2 or 9.7, but certainly not 9.95. Surely that is possible. But we know we have noisy measurements, so we have no reason to think one of the measurements has no noise, or that one person made a gross error that allows us to discard their measurement. Given all available information, the best estimate must be 9.95.
在更复杂的情况下，这种推理方式就显得不合常理了。让我们进一步思考一下。或许，更合理的假设是：其中一个人犯了错误，所以真实的距离应该是 10.2 或 9.7，而不可能是 9.95。当然，这种可能性是存在的。但是，由于我们的测量结果都存在误差，我们没有理由认为其中某个测量结果是完全准确的，或者认为某个人犯了严重的错误，从而可以忽略他的测量结果。根据所有可用的信息，最合理的估计值应该是 9.95。

In the update step of the Kalman filter we are not combining two measurements, but one measurement and the prior, our estimate before incorporating the measurement. We went through this logic for the g-h filter. It doesn't matter if we are incorporating information from two measurements, or a measurement and a prediction, the math is the same.
在卡尔曼滤波器的更新步骤中，我们并非将两次测量结果相加，而是将某次测量结果与先前的估计值相加。对于 g-h 滤波器来说，道理也是一样的。无论我们是结合两次测量结果的信息，还是将某次测量结果与预测值相结合，其数学运算方式都是一样的。

Let's look at that. I'll create a fairly inaccurate prior of N(8.5,1.5)N(8.5,1.5) and a more accurate measurement of N(10.2,0.5).N(10.2,0.5). By "accurate" I mean the sensor variance is smaller than the prior's variance, not that I somehow know that the dog is closer to 10.2 than 8.5. Next I'll plot the reverse relationship: an accurate prior of N(8.5,0.5)N(8.5,0.5) and a inaccurate measurement of N(10.2,1.5)N(10.2,1.5).
让我们来看看。我会为 N(8.5,1.5)N(8.5,1.5) 设定一个相对不准确的初始值，而为 N(10.2,0.5).N(10.2,0.5). 则设定一个更准确的测量值。所谓“准确”，指的是传感器的误差小于初始值的误差，而不是说我知道那只狗的距离确实更接近 10.2 而不是 8.5。接下来，我会绘制出相反的情况：为 N(8.5,0.5)N(8.5,0.5) 设定一个准确的初始值，而为 N(10.2,1.5)N(10.2,1.5) 则设定一个不准确的测量值。

In [15]:  在[15]中：
prior, z = gaussian(8.5, 1.5), gaussian(10.2, 0.5)
plot_products(prior, z)

prior, z = gaussian(8.5, 0.5), gaussian(10.2, 1.5)
plot_products(prior, z)

The result is a Gaussian that is taller than either input. This makes sense - we have incorporated information, so our variance should have been reduced. And notice how the result is far closer to the the input with the smaller variance. We have more confidence in that value, so it makes sense to weight it more heavily.
最终得到的高斯分布比两个输入值都要高。这是合乎逻辑的——因为我们已经把各种信息都考虑进去了，所以方差应该会减小。另外，可以注意到，最终得到的结果更接近于那个方差较小的输入值。我们对那个值的置信度更高，因此给予它更高的权重是合理的。

It seems to work, but is it really correct? There is more to say about this, but I want to get a working filter going so you can experience it in concrete terms. After that we will revisit Gaussian multiplication and determine why it is correct.
看起来这个方法确实有效，但是否真的正确呢？关于这一点还有更多需要探讨的。不过，我现在想先让这个过滤器能够正常运行起来，这样你们就能亲自体验它的效果了。之后，我们再重新探讨高斯乘法的原理，弄清楚为什么这种方法是正确的。

Interactive Example  交互式示例¶

This interactive code provides sliders to alter the mean and variance of two Gaussians that are being multiplied together. As you move the sliders the plot is redrawn. Place your cursor inside the code cell and press CTRL+Enter to execute it.
这段交互式代码提供了滑块，可用于调整两个高斯函数的均值和方差。当您移动滑块时，图表会随之重新绘制。请将光标置于代码单元格内，然后按 CTRL+Enter 键来执行该代码。

In [16]:  在[16]中：
from ipywidgets import interact

def interactive_gaussian(m1, m2, v1, v2):
    g1 = gaussian(m1, v1)
    g2 = gaussian(m2, v2)
    plot_products(g1, g2)
    
interact(interactive_gaussian,
         m1=(5, 10., .5), m2=(10, 15, .5), 
         v1=(.1, 2, .1), v2=(.1, 2, .1));

interactive(children=(FloatSlider(value=7.5, description='m1', max=10.0, min=5.0, step=0.5), FloatSlider(value…

First Kalman Filter  卡尔曼滤波器初探¶

Let's get back to concrete terms and implement a Kalman filter. We've implemented the update() and predict() functions. We just need to write some boilerplate code to simulate a dog and create the measurements. I've put a DogSimulation class in kf_internal to avoid getting distracted with that task.
让我们回到具体的实现上，来运用卡尔曼滤波器吧。我们已经实现了 update() 和 predict() 这两个函数。现在只需要编写一些通用代码来模拟狗的行为并生成相应的测量数据即可。我把 DogSimulation 类放在了 kf_internal 中，这样就不会因为处理这个任务而分心了。

This boilerplate code sets up the problem by definine the means, variances, and generating the simulated dog movement.
这段样板代码通过定义各种参数、计算方差以及生成模拟的狗的移动轨迹，从而确立了整个问题的框架。

In [17]:  在[17]中：
import kf_book.kf_internal as kf_internal
from kf_book.kf_internal import DogSimulation

np.random.seed(13)

process_var = 1. # variance in the dog's movement
sensor_var = 2. # variance in the sensor

x = gaussian(0., 20.**2)  # dog's position, N(0, 20**2)
velocity = 1
dt = 1. # time step in seconds
process_model = gaussian(velocity*dt, process_var) # displacement to add to x
  
# simulate dog and get measurements
dog = DogSimulation(
    x0=x.mean, 
    velocity=process_model.mean, 
    measurement_var=sensor_var, 
    process_var=process_model.var)

# create list of measurements
zs = [dog.move_and_sense() for _ in range(10)]

And here is the Kalman filter.
这就是卡尔曼滤波器。

In [18]:  在[18]中：
print('PREDICT\t\t\tUPDATE')
print('     x      var\t\t  z\t    x      var')

# perform Kalman filter on measurement z
for z in zs:    
    prior = predict(x, process_model)
    likelihood = gaussian(z, sensor_var)
    x = update(prior, likelihood)

    kf_internal.print_gh(prior, x, z)

print()
print(f'final estimate:        {x.mean:10.3f}')
print(f'actual final position: {dog.x:10.3f}')

PREDICT			UPDATE
     x      var		  z	    x      var
  1.000  401.000	1.354	  1.352   1.990
  2.352    2.990	1.882	  2.070   1.198
  3.070    2.198	4.341	  3.736   1.047
  4.736    2.047	7.156	  5.960   1.012
  6.960    2.012	6.939	  6.949   1.003
  7.949    2.003	6.844	  7.396   1.001
  8.396    2.001	9.847	  9.122   1.000
 10.122    2.000	12.553	 11.338   1.000
 12.338    2.000	16.273	 14.305   1.000
 15.305    2.000	14.800	 15.053   1.000

final estimate:            15.053
actual final position:     14.838

Here is an animation of the filter. Predictions are plotted with a red triangle. After the prediction, the filter receives the next measurement, plotted as a black circle. The filter then forms an estimate part way between the two.
以下是该滤波器的动画演示。预测结果以红色三角形表示。在每次预测之后，滤波器会接收下一个测量值，该测量值以黑色圆圈表示。随后，滤波器会在这两个数值之间得出一个估算值。

In [19]:  在[19]中：
from kf_book import book_plots as book_plots
from ipywidgets.widgets import IntSlider

# save output in these lists for plotting
xs, predictions = [], []

process_model = gaussian(velocity, process_var) 

# perform Kalman filter
x = gaussian(0., 20.**2)
for z in zs:    
    prior = predict(x, process_model)
    likelihood = gaussian(z, sensor_var)
    x = update(prior, likelihood)

    # save results
    predictions.append(prior.mean)
    xs.append(x.mean)

def plot_filter(step):
    plt.cla()
    step -= 1
    i = step // 3 + 1
 
    book_plots.plot_predictions(predictions[:i])    
    if step % 3 == 0:
        book_plots.plot_measurements(zs[:i-1])
        book_plots.plot_filter(xs[:i-1])
    elif step % 3 == 1:
        book_plots.plot_measurements(zs[:i])
        book_plots.plot_filter(xs[:i-1])
    else:
        book_plots.plot_measurements(zs[:i])
        book_plots.plot_filter(xs[:i])
    
    plt.xlim(-1, 10)
    plt.ylim(0, 20)
    plt.legend(loc=2);
    plt.show()
    
interact(plot_filter, step=IntSlider(value=1, min=1, max=len(predictions)*3));

interactive(children=(IntSlider(value=1, description='step', max=30, min=1), Output()), _dom_classes=('widget-…

I've plotted the prior (labeled prediction), the measurements, and the filter output. For each iteration of the loop we form a prior, take a measurement, form a likelihood from the measurement, and then incorporate the likelihood into the prior.
我已经绘制出了先验概率值、测量结果以及滤波器的输出结果。在循环的每次迭代中，我们都会先确定先验概率值，然后进行测量，根据测量结果计算出概率值，最后将这个概率值纳入到先验概率值中。

If you look at the plot you can see that the filter estimate is always between the measurement and prediction. Recall that for the g-h filter we argued that the estimate must always be between the measurement and prior. It makes no sense to choose a value outside of the two values. If I predict I am at 10, but measure that I am at 9, it would be foolish to decide that I must be at 8, or 11.
从图表中可以看出，滤波后的估计值始终位于测量值和预测值之间。回想一下，对于 g-h 滤波器来说，我们曾经指出，估计值必须始终处于测量值和先验值之间。选择超出这两个范围的数值是没有意义的。如果我预测自己处于 10 的位置，但实际测量结果却是 9，那么认为自己应该处于 8 或 11 的位置就显得很荒谬了。

Code Walkthrough  代码详解/代码实现过程¶

Now let's walk through the code.
现在让我们来仔细看看这段代码。

process_var = 1.
sensor_var = 2.

These are the variances for the process model and sensor. The meaning of sensor variance should be clear - it is how much variance there is in each measurement. The process variance is how much error there is in the process model. We are predicting that at each time step the dog moves forward one meter. Dogs rarely do what we expect, and things like hills or the whiff of a squirrel will change his progress. If this was a robot responding to digital commands the performance would be much better, and perhaps the variance would be σ2=.05σ2=.05. These are not 'magic' numbers; the square root of the variance is the distance error in meters. It is easy to get a Kalman filter working by just plugging in numbers, but if the numbers do not reflect reality the performance of the filter will be poor.
这些分别是过程模型和传感器的方差值。传感器的方差很容易理解：它指的是每次测量结果之间的差异大小。而过程方差则反映了过程模型中的误差程度。我们预测，狗在每个时间点都会向前移动一米。不过，狗的行为往往不会完全按照我们的预期行事，比如有障碍物或听到松鼠的叫声时，狗的行进路径就会发生变化。如果这是一个响应数字指令的机器人，那么其性能会好得多，相应的方差值也可能接近于 0。这些数字并非“神奇的数值”；方差的平方根实际上代表了误差的大小，单位是米。虽然只要输入正确的数值，卡尔曼滤波器就能正常工作，但如果输入的数值与实际情况不符，滤波器的性能就会很差。

x = gaussian(0., 20.**2)

This is the dog's initial position expressed as a Gaussian. The position is 0 meters, and the variance to 400 m22, which is a standard deviation of 20 meters. You can think of this as saying "I believe with 99.7% accuracy the position is 0 plus or minus 60 meters". This is because with Gaussians ~99.7% of values fall within ±3σ±3σ of the mean.
这就是那只狗的初始位置，用高斯分布来表示。该位置为 0 米，方差为 400 米，相当于标准差为 20 米。可以这样理解： “我有 99.7%的把握认为，该狗的位置在 0 米上下 60 米的范围内。”因为根据高斯分布，99.7%的数值都位于平均值上下 1 个标准差范围内。

process_model = gaussian(velocity, process_var)

This is the process model - the description of how we think the dog moves. How do I know the velocity? Magic? Consider it a prediction, or perhaps we have a secondary velocity sensor. If this is a robot then this would be a control input to the robot. In subsequent chapters we will learn how to handle situations where you don't have a velocity sensor or input, so please accept this simplification for now.
这就是所谓的“过程模型”——也就是我们对狗的移动方式所做出的描述。那我怎么知道狗的速度呢？这有什么神奇的吗？其实可以把这看作是一种预测而已。或者，也许我们还有别的方式来测量速度。如果这是一台机器人，那么这些数据就可以作为机器人的控制输入信号。在后面的章节中，我们将学习如何处理没有速度传感器或相关输入信号的情况。所以，请暂时接受这种简化后的解释吧。

Next we initialize the simulation and create 10 measurements:
接下来，我们初始化模拟过程，并进行 10 次测量：

dog = DogSimulation(
    x0=x.mean, 
    velocity=process_model.mean, 
    measurement_var=sensor_var, 
    process_var=process_model.var)

zs = [dog.move_and_sense() for _ in range(10)]

Now we enter our predict() ... update() loop.
现在，我们进入 predict() ... update() 循环。

for z in zs:
    prior = predict(x, process_model)
    likelihood = gaussian(z, sensor_var)
    x = update(prior, likelihood)

The first time through the loop prior is (1.0, 401.0), as can be seen in the printed table. After the prediction, we believe that we are at 1.0, and the variance is now 401, up from 400. The variance got worse, which is what always happens during the prediction step because it involves a loss of information.
在循环的第一次迭代中， prior 的值为 (1.0, 401.0) ，这一点可以从打印出的表格中看出。在预测之后，我们认为当前的值应为 1.0，而方差则从 400 上升到了 401。方差有所增加，这在预测过程中是不可避免的，因为预测过程本身就会导致信息的丢失。

Then we call the update function using prior as the current position.
然后，我们以 prior 作为当前位置，调用更新函数。

For this I get this as the result: pos = (1.352, 1.990), z = 1.354.
因此，我得到的结果是： pos = (1.352, 1.990), z = 1.354 。

What is happening? The dog is actually at 1.0 but the measured position is 1.354 due to sensor noise. That is pretty far from the predicted value of 1. The variance of the prior is 401 m22. A large variance implies that confidence is very low, so the filter estimates the position to be very close to the measurement: 1.352.
发生了什么？实际上，狗的位置应该是 1.0，但由于传感器噪声的影响，实际测量值为 1.354。这个数值与预测值 1 相差很大。先验值的方差为 401 米。较大的方差意味着置信度很低，因此滤波器将位置估计为与测量值非常接近的 1.352。

Now look at the variance: 1.99 m22. It has dropped tremendously from 401 m22. Why? Well, the RFID has a reasonably small variance of 2.0 m22, so we trust it far more than the prior. However, the previous belief does contain a bit of useful information, so our variance is now slightly smaller than 2.0.
现在来看看方差：1.99 百万 22 。这一数值相比 401 百万 22 有了大幅下降。为什么呢？因为 RFID 的方差相对较小，仅为 2.0 百万 22 ，因此我们更信任 RFID 的测量结果。不过，之前的测量结果也包含了一些有用的信息，所以现在的方差略小于 2.0 百万。

Now the software loops, calling predict() and update() in turn. By the end the final estimated position is 15.053 vs the actual position of 14.838. The variance has converged to 1.0 m22.
现在，该软件会循环执行，依次调用 predict() 和 update() 。最终，估算出的位置为 15.053，而实际位置为 14.838。偏差已收敛到 1.0 米以内，即 22 所表示的数值。

Now look at the plot. The noisy measurements are plotted with black circles, and the filter results are drawn with a solid blue line. Both are quite noisy, but notice how much noisier the measurements are. I plotted the prediction (prior) with red triangles. The estimate always lies between the prior and the measurement. This is your first Kalman filter and it seems to work!
现在请看这个图表。带有噪声的测量数据用黑色圆点表示，而经过过滤后的结果则用蓝色实线表示。两者都存在相当大的噪声，但可以明显看出，测量数据的噪声要大得多。我用红色三角形标出了预测值。可以看出，预测值始终位于测量值和先验值之间。这就是第一个卡尔曼滤波器的应用实例，看起来它确实有效！

The filtering is implemented in only a few lines of code. Most of the code is either initialization, storing of data, simulating the dog movement, and printing results. The code that performs the filtering is very succinct:
过滤功能的实现仅需要几行代码而已。大部分代码用于初始化、数据存储、模拟狗的移动以及结果输出。负责执行过滤操作的代码非常简洁：

prior = predict(x, process_model)
likelihood = gaussian(z, sensor_var)
x = update(prior, likelihood)

If we didn't use the predict and update functions the code might be:
如果我们不使用 predict 和 update 函数，那么代码可能会是这样的：

for z in zs:
    # predict
    dx = velocity*dt
    pos = pos + dx
    var = var + process_var

    # update
    pos  = (var*z + sensor_var*pos) / (var + sensor_var)
    var = (var * sensor_var) / (var + sensor_var)

Just 5 lines of very simple math implements the entire filter!
只需要 5 行非常简单的数学公式，就能实现整个过滤过程！

In this example I only plotted 10 data points so the output from the print statements would not overwhelm us. Now let's look at the filter's performance with more data. The variance is plotted as a lightly shaded yellow area between dotted lines. I've increased the size of the process and sensor variance so they are easier to see on the chart - for a real Kalman filter of course you will not be randomly changing these values.
在这个例子中，我只绘制了 10 个数据点，这样，打印出来的结果就不会过于复杂难懂。现在，让我们来看看当数据量增加时，该滤波器的性能如何。方差以浅黄色区域的形式显示在虚线之间。为了便于观察，我把过程方差和传感器方差的数值放大了——当然，在实际的卡尔曼滤波器中，这些数值是不会随意变化的。

In [20]:  在[20]中：
process_var = 2.
sensor_var = 4.5
x = gaussian(0., 400.)
process_model = gaussian(1., process_var)
N = 25

dog = DogSimulation(x.mean, process_model.mean, sensor_var, process_var)
zs = [dog.move_and_sense() for _ in range(N)]

xs, priors = np.zeros((N, 2)), np.zeros((N, 2))
for i, z in enumerate(zs):
    prior = predict(x, process_model)    
    x = update(prior, gaussian(z, sensor_var))
    priors[i] = prior
    
    xs[i] = x

book_plots.plot_measurements(zs)
book_plots.plot_filter(xs[:, 0], var=priors[:, 1])
book_plots.plot_predictions(priors[:, 0])
book_plots.show_legend()
kf_internal.print_variance(xs)

	4.4502 2.6507 2.2871 2.1955 2.1712
	2.1647 2.1629 2.1625 2.1623 2.1623
	2.1623 2.1623 2.1623 2.1623 2.1623
	2.1623 2.1623 2.1623 2.1623 2.1623
	2.1623 2.1623 2.1623 2.1623 2.1623

Here we can see that the variance converges to 2.1623 in 9 steps. This means that we have become very confident in our position estimate. It is equal to σ=1.47σ=1.47 meters. Contrast this to the sensor's σ=2.12σ=2.12 meters. The first few measurements are unsure due to our uncertainty of the initial position, but the filter quickly converges to an estimate with lower variance than the sensor!
我们可以看到，经过 9 次迭代后，方差值收敛到了 2.1623。这意味着我们对位置的估计已经相当准确了。该数值相当于 σ=1.47σ=1.47 米。与传感器的 σ=2.12σ=2.12 米相比，我们的估计结果更为精确。由于初始位置的不确定性，最初的几次测量结果并不可靠，但经过过滤处理后，得到的估计值方差明显小于传感器的测量结果！

This code fully implements a Kalman filter. If you have tried to read the literature you are perhaps surprised, because this looks nothing like the endless pages of math in those books. So long as we worry about using the equations rather than deriving them the topic is approachable. Moreover, I hope you'll agree that you have a decent intuitive grasp of what is happening. We represent beliefs with Gaussians, and they get better over time because more measurements means we have more data to work with.
这段代码完整实现了卡尔曼滤波器。如果你尝试过阅读相关文献，可能会感到惊讶：因为这里的实现方式与那些书中冗长的数学公式完全不同。只要我们专注于使用这些公式，而不用去推导它们，那么这个概念就很容易理解了。此外，我相信你也能感觉到，自己对整个过程的直觉理解相当不错。我们用高斯分布来表示各种概率值，随着时间的推移，这些概率值会越来越准确，因为更多的测量数据意味着我们有更多的信息可以用来进行计算。

Exercise: Modify Variance Values
练习：修改方差数值¶

Modify the values of process_var and sensor_var and note the effect on the filter and on the variance. Which has a larger effect on the variance convergence? For example, which results in a smaller variance:
修改 process_var 和 sensor_var 的值，观察其对滤波效果以及方差的影响。其中，哪一个因素对方差收敛的影响更大呢？也就是说，哪一个方案能使得方差更小呢？

process_var = 40
sensor_var = 2

or:  或者：

process_var = 2
sensor_var = 40

KF Animation  KF 动画¶

If you are reading this in a browser you will be able to see an animation of the filter tracking the dog directly below this sentence.
如果您是通过浏览器阅读这段文字，那么您会看到一个动画，展示该过滤器是如何跟踪那只狗的。就在这句话的正下方。

The top plot shows the output of the filter in green, and the measurements with a dashed red line. The bottom plot shows the Gaussian at each step.
上图以绿色线条显示了滤波器的处理结果，用红色虚线则标出了测量值。下图则展示了每一步处理后的高斯分布情况。

When the track first starts you can see that the measurements varies quite a bit from the initial prediction. At this point the Gaussian probability is small (the curve is low and wide) so the filter does not trust its prediction. As a result, the filter adjusts its estimate a large amount. As the filter innovates you can see that as the Gaussian becomes taller, indicating greater certainty in the estimate, the filter's output becomes very close to a straight line. At x = 15 and greater you can see that there is a large amount of noise in the measurement, but the filter does not react much to it compared to how much it changed for the first noisy measurement.
当测量开始时，可以看到实际数值与最初的预测值有很大差异。此时，高斯曲线的幅度较小（曲线较平缓），因此滤波器对自身的预测结果不太信任。相应地，滤波器会大幅调整自己的估算值。随着滤波器不断更新其估算结果，高斯曲线会变得更高，这意味着估算的准确性有所提高，滤波器的输出也越接近平直线。在 x = 15 及更高的数值范围内，虽然测量结果中存在大量噪声，但与第一次遇到噪声时的情况相比，滤波器的反应并不明显。

Kalman Gain  卡尔曼增益¶

We see that the filter works. Now let's go back to the math to understand what is happening. The posterior xx is computed as the likelihood times the prior (Lx¯Lx¯), where both are Gaussians.
我们看到，该滤波器确实有效。现在，让我们回到数学公式上来，弄清楚其中的具体原理。后验概率 xx 是由似然值与先验概率 Lx¯Lx¯ 相乘得到的，而这两者都是高斯分布。

Therefore the mean of the posterior is given by:
因此，后验概率的均值如下：

μ=σ¯2μz+σ2zμ¯σ¯2+σ2zμ=σ¯2μz+σz2μ¯σ¯2+σz2

I use the subscript zz to denote the measurement. We can rewrite this as:
我用下标 zz 来表示该测量值。我们可以将其重新表示为：

μ=(σ¯2σ¯2+σ2z)μz+(σ2zσ¯2+σ2z)μ¯μ=(σ¯2σ¯2+σz2)μz+(σz2σ¯2+σz2)μ¯

In this form it is easy to see that we are scaling the measurement and the prior by weights:
从这个形式可以看出，我们是通过权重来调整测量值和先验值的。

μ=W1μz+W2μ¯μ=W1μz+W2μ¯

The weights sum to one because the denominator is a normalization term. We introduce a new term, K=W1K=W1, giving us:
各权重之和为一，因为分母是一个用于归一化的项。我们引入了一个新项 K=W1K=W1 ，从而得到：

μ=Kμz+(1−K)μ¯=μ¯+K(μz−μ¯)μ=Kμz+(1−K)μ¯=μ¯+K(μz−μ¯)

where  在……的地方/在何处

K=σ¯2σ¯2+σ2zK=σ¯2σ¯2+σz2

KK is the Kalman gain. It's the crux of the Kalman filter. It is a scaling term that chooses a value partway between μzμz and μ¯μ¯.
KK 就是卡尔曼增益。它是卡尔曼滤波器的核心要素。实际上，它是一个用于调整数值的参数，其取值介于 μzμz 和 μ¯μ¯ 之间。

Let's work a few examples. If the measurement is nine times more accurate than the prior, then σ¯2=9σ2zσ¯2=9σz2, and
我们来看几个例子吧。如果现在的测量精度是之前的九倍，那么 σ¯2=9σ2zσ¯2=9σz2 ，而……

μ=9σ2zμz+σ2zμ¯9σ2z+σ2z=(910)μz+(110)μ¯μ=9σz2μz+σz2μ¯9σz2+σz2=(910)μz+(110)μ¯

Hence K=910K=910, and to form the posterior we take nine tenths of the measurement and one tenth of the prior.
因此， K=910K=910 。在计算后验概率时，我们取测量值的九成，以及先验概率的十分之一。

If the measurement and prior are equally accurate, then σ¯2=σ2zσ¯2=σz2 and
如果测量结果和先前的数据都同样准确的话，那么 σ¯2=σ2zσ¯2=σz2 和……

μ=σ2z(μ¯+μz)2σ2z=(12)μ¯+(12)μzμ=σz2(μ¯+μz)2σz2=(12)μ¯+(12)μz

which is the average of the two means. It makes intuitive sense to take the average of two equally accurate values.
这是两个平均值的平均值。从直觉上来说，对两个同样准确的数值取平均值是合理的。

We can also express the variance in terms of the Kalman gain:
我们还可以用卡尔曼增益来表示方差：

σ2=σ¯2σ2zσ¯2+σ2z=Kσ2z=(1−K)σ¯2σ2=σ¯2σz2σ¯2+σz2=Kσz2=(1−K)σ¯2

We can understand this by looking at this chart:
我们可以通过查看这张图表来理解这一点：

In [21]:  在[21]中：
import kf_book.book_plots as book_plots
book_plots.show_residual_chart()

The Kalman gain KK is a scale factor that chooses a value along the residual. This leads to an alternative but equivalent implementation for update() and predict():
卡尔曼增益 KK 其实是一个比例因子，用于确定残差值的大小。这样一来， update() 和 predict() 的实现方式虽然有所不同，但实际上是等效的。

In [22]:  在[22]中：
def update(prior, measurement):
    x, P = prior        # mean and variance of prior
    z, R = measurement  # mean and variance of measurement
    
    y = z - x        # residual
    K = P / (P + R)  # Kalman gain

    x = x + K*y      # posterior
    P = (1 - K) * P  # posterior variance
    return gaussian(x, P)

def predict(posterior, movement):
    x, P = posterior # mean and variance of posterior
    dx, Q = movement # mean and variance of movement
    x = x + dx
    P = P + Q
    return gaussian(x, P)

Why have I written it in this form, and why have I chosen these terrible variable names? A few related reasons. A majority of books and papers present the Kalman filter in this form. My derivation of the filter from Bayesian principles is not unknown, but it is not used nearly as often. Alternative derivations naturally lead to this form of the equations. Also, the equations for the multivariate Kalman filter look almost exactly like these equations. So, you need to learn and understand them.
我为什么要用这种形式来表达卡尔曼滤波器呢？又为什么要选择这么糟糕的变量名称呢？其实有几个原因。大多数书籍和论文都是以这种形式来介绍卡尔曼滤波器的。虽然我也可以从贝叶斯原理来推导卡尔曼滤波器，但这种推导方式并不常用。其他推导方式也都会得到与这种形式相同的方程。所以，人们必须学会并理解这种表达方式。

Where do the names z, P, Q, and R come from? You will see them used in the rest of this book. In the literature RR is nearly universally used for the measurement noise, QQ for the process noise and PP for the variance of the state. Using zz for the measurement is common, albeit not universal. Almost every book and paper you read will use these variable names. Get used to them.
z 、 P 、 Q 和 R 这些符号是从哪里来的呢？在本书的其余部分中，你还会经常看到这些符号的用法。在相关文献中， RR 通常用来表示测量噪声， QQ 用来表示过程噪声，而 PP 则用来表示状态的方差。虽然并非所有文献都如此，但用 zz 来表示测量结果的做法相当普遍。你几乎会在所有书籍和论文中看到这些符号的用法。请尽快熟悉它们吧。

This is also a powerful way to think about filtering. This is the way we reasoned about the g-h filter. It emphasizes taking the residual y=μz−μ¯y=μz−μ¯, finding the Kalman gain as a ratio of our uncertainty in the prior and measurement K=P/(P+R)K=P/(P+R), and computing the posterior by adding KyKy to the prior.
这也是思考滤波问题的一种有效方式。我们正是用这种方式来处理 g-h 滤波器的。其核心思路是：先计算残差 y=μz−μ¯y=μz−μ¯ ，再以先验信息中的不确定性与测量值 K=P/(P+R)K=P/(P+R) 之比来确定卡尔曼增益，最后通过将 KyKy 加到先验信息上来得到后验信息。

The Bayesian aspect is obscured in this form, as is the fact that we are multiplying the likelihood by the prior. Both viewpoints are equivalent because the math is identical. I chose the Bayesian approach because I think it give a much more intuitive yet deep understanding of the probabilistic reasoning. This alternative form using KK gives a deep understanding of what is known as the orthogonal projection approach. Dr. Kalman used that derivation, not Bayesian reasoning, when he invented this filter. You will understand more about this in the next few chapters.
在这种表达方式中，贝叶斯方法的本质被忽略了，同样被忽略的还有“我们将概率值与先验概率相乘”这一事实。不过，这两种观点其实是等价的，因为其数学表达方式完全相同。我选择采用贝叶斯方法，是因为我觉得它能让我们更直观、更深入地理解概率推理的原理。而使用 KK 的这种表达方式，则有助于我们深入理解所谓的“正交投影法”。卡尔曼博士在发明这种滤波器时，使用的是正交投影法，而非贝叶斯推理。在接下来的几章中，你会对此有更深入的了解。

Full Description of the Algorithm
该算法的完整描述¶

Recall the diagram we used for the g-h filter:
请回想一下我们用来描述 g-h 滤波器的那个图表：

We've been doing the same thing in this chapter. The Kalman filter makes a prediction, takes a measurement, and then forms a new estimate somewhere between the two.
在这一章中，我们所做的其实都是一样的。卡尔曼滤波器会先进行预测，再获取实际测量值，最后根据这两个数值来得出一个新的估计值。

This is extremely important to understand: Every filter in this book implements the same algorithm, just with different mathematical details. The math can become challenging in later chapters, but the idea is easy to understand.
这一点非常重要：本书中的每一个过滤器都采用了相同的算法，只不过在数学细节上有所区别而已。后面的章节中，相关的数学公式可能会比较复杂，但核心思想其实很容易理解。

It is important to see past the details of the equations of a specific filter and understand what the equations are calculating and why. There are a tremendous number of filters. They all use different math to implement the same algorithm. The choice of math affects the quality of results and what problems can be represented, but not the underlying ideas.
重要的是，不要只盯着某个特定滤波器所对应的方程式的细节，而应理解这些方程式究竟在计算什么，以及为何要这样计算。世界上有无数种滤波器，它们虽然都使用不同的数学公式来实现相同的算法，但所选的数学公式会影响到最终的结果质量以及该滤波器能够处理的问题类型。不过，这些数学公式的选择并不会影响滤波器的基本原理。

Here is the generic algorithm:
以下是该算法的通用流程：

Initialization  初始化

1. Initialize the state of the filter
2. Initialize our belief in the state

Predict  预测

1. Use system behavior to predict state at the next time step
2. Adjust belief to account for the uncertainty in prediction

Update  更新

1. Get a measurement and associated belief about its accuracy
2. Compute residual between estimated state and measurement
3. Compute scaling factor based on whether the measurement
or prediction is more accurate
4. set state between the prediction and measurement based 
on scaling factor
5. update belief in the state based on how certain we are 
in the measurement

You will be hard pressed to find a Bayesian filter algorithm that does not fit into this form. Some filters will not include some aspects, such as error in the prediction, and others will have very complicated methods of computation, but this is what they all do.
很难找到一种不符合这种模式的贝叶斯滤波算法。有些滤波器会忽略某些因素，比如预测误差；而另一些则采用极其复杂的计算方法。但归根结底，所有贝叶斯滤波算法都是如此运作的。

The equations for the univariate Kalman filter are:
单变量卡尔曼滤波器的方程如下：

Predict  预测

Equationx¯=x+fxImplementationμ¯=μ+μfxσ¯2=σ2+σ2fxKalman Formx¯=x+dxP¯=P+QEquationImplementationKalman Formx¯=x+fxμ¯=μ+μfxx¯=x+dxσ¯2=σ2+σfx2P¯=P+Q

Update  更新

Equationx=∥Lx¯∥Implementationy=z−μ¯K=σ¯2σ¯2+σ2zμ=μ¯+Kyσ2=σ¯2σ2zσ¯2+σ2zKalman Formy=z−x¯K=P¯P¯+Rx=x¯+KyP=(1−K)P¯EquationImplementationKalman Formx=‖Lx¯‖y=z−μ¯y=z−x¯K=σ¯2σ¯2+σz2K=P¯P¯+Rμ=μ¯+Kyx=x¯+Kyσ2=σ¯2σz2σ¯2+σz2P=(1−K)P¯

Comparison with g-h and discrete Bayes Filters
与 g-h 滤波器及离散贝叶斯滤波器的比较¶

Now is a good time to understand the differences between these three filters in terms of how we model errors. For the g-h filter we modeled our measurements as shown in this graph:
现在正是了解这三种滤波器在处理误差方式上的差异的好时机。对于 g-h 滤波器，我们按照下图所示的方式对测量数据进行了处理：

In [23]:  在[23]中：
book_plots.plot_errorbars([(160, 3, 'A'), (170, 9, 'B')], xlims=(150, 180))

Sensor A returned a measurement of 160, and sensor B returned 170. The bars are error bars - they illustrate the possible range of error for the measurement. Hence, the actual value that A is measuring can be between 157 to 163, and B is measuring a value between 161 to 179.
传感器 A 的测量值为 160，传感器 B 的测量值为 170。那些条形图表示的是误差范围——它们反映了测量结果可能存在的误差幅度。因此，传感器 A 的实际测量值应该在 157 到 163 之间，而传感器 B 的实际测量值则应在 161 到 179 之间。

I did not define it at the time, but this is a uniform distribution. A uniform distribution assigns equal probability to any event in the range. According to this model it is equally likely for sensor A to read 157, 160, or 163. Any value outside these ranges have 0 probability.
当时我并没有明确给出其定义，但实际上这是一种均匀分布。所谓均匀分布，指的是在该范围内，所有事件被选中的概率都是相等的。根据这一模型，传感器 A 读出 157、160 或 163 的概率都是相同的。而那些超出这个范围的数值，被选中的概率则为 0。

We can model this situation with Gaussians. I'll use N(160,32)N(160,32) for sensor A, and N(170,92)N(170,92) for sensor B. I've plotted these below with the uniform distribution error bars for comparison.
我们可以用高斯函数来模拟这种情况。我用 N(160,32)N(160,32) 表示传感器 A，用 N(170,92)N(170,92) 表示传感器 B。下图中，我还标出了均匀分布的误差范围，以便进行比较。

In [24]:  在[24]中：
xs = np.arange(145, 190, 0.1)
ys = [stats.gaussian(x, 160, 3**2) for x in xs]
plt.plot(xs, ys, label='A', color='g')

ys = [stats.gaussian(x, 170, 9**2) for x in xs]
plt.plot(xs, ys, label='B', color='b')
plt.legend();
plt.errorbar(160, [0.04], xerr=[3], fmt='o', color='g', capthick=2, capsize=10)    
plt.errorbar(170, [0.015], xerr=[9], fmt='o', color='b', capthick=2, capsize=10);

Using a uniform or Gaussian distribution is a modeling choice. Neither exactly describes reality. In most cases the Gaussian distribution is more realistic. Most sensors are more likely to return readings near the value being measured, and unlikely to return a reading far from that value. The Gaussian models this tendency. In contrast the uniform distribution assumes that any measurement within a range is equally likely.
选择使用均匀分布或高斯分布其实是一种建模上的取舍。这两种分布都无法完全反映现实情况。在大多数情况下，高斯分布更符合实际情况。大多数传感器所测得的数值往往接近于被测量的真实值，而很少会测得与真实值相差很大的数值。高斯分布正好能够体现这种趋势。相比之下，均匀分布则假设在某个范围内，任何数值被测得的可能性都是相等的。

Now let's see the discrete distribution used in the discrete Bayes filter. This model divides the range of possible values into discrete ranges and assigns a probability to each bucket. This assignment can be entirely arbitrary so long as the probabilities sum to one.
现在，让我们来看看在离散贝叶斯滤波器中使用的离散分布。该模型将所有可能的数值范围划分为若干个离散的区间，并为每个区间分配一个概率值。只要这些概率值之和等于 1，这种分配方式完全可以由人为决定。

Let's plot the data for one sensor using a uniform distribution, a Gaussian distribution, and a discrete distribution.
让我们用均匀分布、高斯分布和离散分布来分别绘制某个传感器的数据曲线吧。

In [25]:  在[25]中：
from random import random
xs = np.arange(145, 190, 0.1)
ys = [stats.gaussian(x, 160, 3**2) for x in xs]
belief = np.array([random() for _ in range(40)])
belief = belief / sum(belief)

x = np.linspace(155, 165, len(belief))
plt.gca().bar(x, belief, width=0.2)
plt.plot(xs, ys, label='A', color='g')
plt.errorbar(160, [0.04], xerr=[3], fmt='o', color='k', capthick=2, capsize=10)    
plt.xlim(150, 170);

I used random numbers to form the discrete distribution to illustrate that it can model any arbitrary probability distribution. This provides it with enormous power. With enough discrete buckets we can model the error characteristics of any sensor no matter how complicated. But with this power comes mathematical intractability. Multiplying or adding Gaussians takes two lines of math, and the result is another Gaussian. This regularity allows us to perform powerful analysis on the performance and behavior of our filters. Multiplying or adding a discrete distribution requires looping over the data, and we have no easy way to characterize the result. Analyzing the performance characteristics of a filter based on a discrete distribution is extremely difficult to impossible.
我使用随机数来构建离散分布，以此说明该模型能够用来模拟任何形式的概率分布。这一特性使得该模型具有极大的实用性。只要拥有足够多的离散区间，我们就能模拟出任何复杂传感器的误差特性。不过，这种强大功能也带来了数学上的复杂性：对高斯分布进行运算时，只需执行两步数学操作，结果依然是高斯分布。这种规律性让我们能够轻松分析滤波器的性能和行为。但对于离散分布来说，情况则完全不同：对其进行运算需要遍历所有数据，而且我们很难准确描述运算结果。基于离散分布来分析滤波器的性能特性，几乎是不可能的。

There is no 'correct' choice here. Later in the book we will introduce the particle filter which uses a discrete distribution. It is an extremely powerful technique because it can handle arbitrarily complex situations. This comes at the cost of slow performance, and resistance to analytical analysis.
这里并没有所谓的“正确”选择。在本书的后面部分，我们将介绍使用离散分布的粒子滤波算法。这是一种极其强大的技术，因为它能够处理各种复杂的情形。不过，这种方法的缺点是计算速度较慢，且难以进行数学分析。

For now we will ignore these matters and return to using Gaussians for the next several chapters. As we progress you will learn the strengths and limitations of using Gaussians in our mathematical models.
目前，我们先忽略这些问题，继续在接下来的几章中使用高斯函数。随着学习的深入，你将会了解在数学模型中使用高斯函数的优点和局限性。

Introduction to Designing a Filter
滤波器设计入门¶

So far we have developed filters for a position sensor. We are used to this problem by now, and may feel ill-equipped to implement a Kalman filter for a different problem. To be honest, there is still quite a bit of information missing from this presentation. Following chapters will fill in the gaps. Still, let's get a feel for it by designing and implementing a Kalman filter for a thermometer. The sensor for the thermometer outputs a voltage that corresponds to the temperature that is being measured. We have read the manufacturer's specifications for the sensor, and it tells us that the sensor exhibits white noise with a standard deviation of 0.13 volts.
到目前为止，我们已经为位置传感器开发了相应的滤波器。对于这类问题，我们已经很熟悉了；不过，要将其应用到其他类型的问题的处理上，可能就需要使用卡尔曼滤波器了。说实话，目前的描述中还缺少不少信息。接下来的章节会补充这些遗漏的内容。不过，我们不妨先试着为温度计设计并实现一个卡尔曼滤波器，从而了解其原理。温度计的传感器会输出与所测温度相对应的电压值。我们已经查阅了该传感器的制造商规格书，得知该传感器产生的噪声属于白噪声，其标准差为 0.13 伏特。

We can simulate the temperature sensor measurement with this function:
我们可以利用这个功能来模拟温度传感器的测量结果：

In [26]:  在[26]中：
def volt(voltage, std):
    return voltage + (randn() * std)

Now we need to write the Kalman filter processing loop. As with our previous problem, we need to perform a cycle of predicting and updating. The sensing step probably seems clear - call volt() to get the measurement, pass the result into update() method, but what about the predict step? We do not have a sensor to detect 'movement' in the voltage, and for any small duration we expect the voltage to remain constant. How shall we handle this?
现在，我们需要编写卡尔曼滤波器的处理流程。与之前的问题类似，我们需要进行预测和更新的操作。传感步骤其实很简单：调用 volt() 来获取测量结果，然后将结果传递给 update() 方法。但是，预测步骤该如何处理呢？我们没有传感器来检测电压中的“变化”，而且，在短时间内，我们预期电压会保持不变。那我们该如何处理这种情况呢？

As always, we will trust in the math. We have no known movement, so we will set that to zero. However, that means that we are predicting that the temperature will never change. If that is true, then over time we should become extremely confident in our results. Once the filter has enough measurements it will become very confident that it can predict the subsequent temperatures, and this will lead it to ignoring measurements that result due to an actual temperature change. This is called a smug filter, and is something you want to avoid. So we will add a bit of error to our prediction step to tell the filter not to discount changes in voltage over time. In the code below I set process_var = .05**2. This is the expected variance in the change of voltage over each time step. I chose this value merely to be able to show how the variance changes through the update and predict steps. For a real sensor you would set this value for the actual amount of change you expect. For example, this would be an extremely small number if it is a thermometer for ambient air temperature in a house, and a high number if this is a thermocouple in a chemical reaction chamber. We will say more about selecting the actual value in the later chapters.
像往常一样，我们会依赖数学公式来进行预测。由于没有观测到任何温度变化，我们将该数值设为零。不过，这意味着我们预测的是温度将永远保持不变。如果真是如此，那么随着时间的推移，我们对预测结果的信心会越来越高。一旦传感器收集了足够的测量数据，它就能非常准确地预测后续的温度变化，从而忽略那些由实际温度变化所导致的测量结果。这种机制被称为“自满型滤波器”，显然这是需要避免的。因此，我们会在预测过程中加入一定的误差，从而让滤波器不会忽视随时间产生的电压变化。在下面的代码中，我将 process_var = .05**2 设置为电压在每个时间步长内的预期变化幅度。我选择这个数值只是为了便于展示在更新和预测过程中，该数值是如何变化的。对于真实的传感器来说，这个数值应设置为实际的电压变化幅度。正如你所预期的那样。例如，如果这是用于测量室内空气温度的温湿度计，那么这个数值会非常小；而如果这是用于测量化学反应室内温度的热电偶，那么数值则会很大。关于如何选择合适的数值，我们将在后面的章节中详细说明。

Let's see what happens.
让我们看看会发生什么吧。

In [27]:  在[27]中：
temp_change = 0
voltage_std = .13
process_var = .05**2
actual_voltage = 16.3

x = gaussian(25., 1000.) # initial state
process_model = gaussian(0., process_var)

N = 50
zs = [volt(actual_voltage, voltage_std) for i in range(N)]
ps = []
estimates = []

for z in zs:
    prior = predict(x, process_model)
    x = update(prior, gaussian(z, voltage_std**2))

    # save for latter plotting
    estimates.append(x.mean)
    ps.append(x.var)

# plot the filter output and the variance
book_plots.plot_measurements(zs)
book_plots.plot_filter(estimates, var=np.array(ps))
book_plots.show_legend()
plt.ylim(16, 17)
book_plots.set_labels(x='step', y='volts')
plt.show()
    
plt.plot(ps)
plt.title('Variance')
print(f'Variance converges to {ps[-1]:.3f}')

Variance converges to 0.005

The first plot shows the individual sensor measurements vs the filter output. Despite a lot of noise in the sensor we quickly discover the approximate voltage of the sensor. In the run I just completed at the time of authorship, the last voltage output from the filter is 16.21316.213, which is quite close to the 16.416.4 used by the volt() function. On other runs I have gotten larger and smaller results.
第一张图表展示了各个传感器的测量值与滤波器输出值之间的关系。尽管传感器产生的噪声很大，但我们仍然能够大致确定传感器的电压值。在作者进行测试时，滤波器输出的最后一个电压值为 16.21316.213 ，这一数值与 volt() 函数所使用的 16.416.4 数值非常接近。在其他测试中，得到的数值则有所差异。

Spec sheets are what they sound like - specifications. Any individual sensor will exhibit different performance based on normal manufacturing variations. Values are often maximums - the spec is a guarantee that the performance will be at least that good. If you buy an expensive piece of equipment it often comes with a sheet of paper displaying the test results of your specific item; this is usually very trustworthy. On the other hand, if this is a cheap sensor it is likely it received little to no testing prior to being sold. Manufacturers typically test a small subset of their output to verify that a sample falls within the desired performance range. If you have a critical application you will need to read the specification sheet carefully to figure out exactly what they mean by their ranges. Do they guarantee their number is a maximum, or is it, say, the 3σ3σ error rate? Is every item tested? Is the variance normal, or some other distribution? Finally, manufacturing is not perfect. Your part might be defective and not match the performance on the sheet.
技术规格表顾名思义，就是对产品各项性能的详细说明。由于制造过程中的不可避免的差异，每个传感器的实际性能都会有所不同。所列数值通常为最大值——技术规格表实际上是一种保证，确保产品的性能至少能达到该水平。如果你购买的是昂贵的设备，通常会附有一份详细记录该设备测试结果的表格；这类数据通常相当可靠。不过，如果是一款便宜的传感器，那么在销售之前很可能几乎没有经过任何测试。制造商通常只对少量样品进行测试，以确保其性能符合要求。如果你需要将这款传感器用于关键应用中，就必须仔细阅读技术规格表，弄清楚各项数值的具体含义。这些数值是最大值吗？还是比如 3σ3σ 错误率的数值呢？所有的产品都经过测试了吗？该方差是否服从正态分布，还是属于其他某种分布呢？此外，制造过程并不完美。你收到的产品可能存在缺陷，其性能可能不符合说明书上的描述。

For example, I am looking at a data sheet for an airflow sensor. There is a field Repeatability, with the value ±0.50%±0.50%. Is this a Gaussian? Is there a bias? For example, perhaps the repeatability is nearly 0.0%0.0% at low temperatures, and always nearly +0.50%+0.50% at high temperatures. Data sheets for electrical components often contain a section of "Typical Performance Characteristics". These are used to capture information that cannot be easily conveyed in a table. For example, I am looking at a chart showing output voltage vs current for a LM555 timer. There are three curves showing the performance at different temperatures. The response is ideally linear, but all three lines are curved. This clarifies that errors in voltage outputs are probably not Gaussian - in this chip's case higher temperatures lead to lower voltage output, and the voltage output is quite nonlinear if the input current is very high.
例如，我正在查看一款气流传感器的数据手册。其中有一个名为“重复性”的参数，其数值为 ±0.50%±0.50% 。这个数值是否服从高斯分布呢？是否存在偏差呢？比如，低温时，重复性可能接近 0.0%0.0% ；而高温时则始终接近 +0.50%+0.50% 。电气元件的数据手册中通常会有一节名为“典型性能特性”的内容。这些内容用于描述那些无法通过表格轻易呈现的信息。例如，我看到的这张图表展示了 LM555 定时器的输出电压与电流之间的关系。图表中有三条曲线，分别对应不同温度下的性能表现。理想情况下，输出电压应呈线性关系，但实际上这三条曲线都是弯曲的。这说明，该芯片的输出电压误差很可能不服从高斯分布——在高温环境下，输出电压会降低；而当输入电流很大时，输出电压的非线性程度也很高。

As you might guess, modeling the performance of your sensors is one of the harder parts of creating a Kalman filter that performs well.
正如你可能猜到的那样，对传感器的性能进行建模，是构建一个性能良好的卡尔曼滤波器过程中较为困难的部分之一。

Animation  动画¶

For those reading this in a browser here is an animation showing the filter working. If you are not using a browser you can see this plot at https://github.com/rlabbe/Kalman-and-Bayesian-Filters-in-Python/blob/master/animations/05_volt_animate.gif.
对于那些在浏览器中阅读此内容的人士，这里有一个动画，展示了该滤波器的工作原理。如果您不是通过浏览器来查看，请访问 https://github.com/rlabbe/Kalman-and-Bayesian-Filters-in-Python/blob/master/animations/05_volt_animate.gif 来查看该动画。

The top plot in the animation draws a green line for the predicted next voltage, then a red '+' for the actual measurement, draws a light red line to show the residual, and then draws a blue line to the filter's output. You can see that when the filter starts the corrections made are quite large, but after only a few updates the filter only adjusts its output by a small amount even when the measurement is far from it.
在动画中，最上方的图表首先用绿色线条表示预测出的下一电压值，然后用红色“+”号表示实际测量值。接着，用浅红色线条表示误差值，最后用蓝色线条表示滤波器的输出值。可以看出，当滤波器开始进行修正时，修正幅度相当大；但经过几次调整后，即使测量值与预测值相差很大，滤波器对输出值的调整幅度也变得很小了。

The lower plot shows the Gaussian belief as the filter innovates. When the filter starts the Gaussian curve is centered over 25, our initial guess for the voltage, and is very wide and short due to our initial uncertainty. But as the filter innovates, the Gaussian quickly moves to about 16.0 and becomes taller, reflecting the growing confidence that the filter has in it's estimate for the voltage. You will also note that the Gaussian's height bounces up and down a little bit. If you watch closely you will see that the Gaussian becomes a bit shorter and more spread out during the prediction step, and becomes taller and narrower as the filter incorporates another measurement.
下图展示了随着滤波器不断更新，高斯分布形态的变化情况。在滤波器开始工作时，高斯曲线的中心位于 25 处，这正是我们对电压的初始估计值。由于初始时存在不确定性，高斯曲线既宽又短。但随着滤波器不断更新，高斯曲线的中心迅速移至 16.0 左右，同时曲线也变得更陡峭，这反映出滤波器对电压估计值的信心越来越强。另外，您还会注意到高斯曲线的高度会有些波动。仔细观察会发现，在预测阶段，高斯曲线会变得更短且更分散；而当滤波器再次获取新的测量数据后，高斯曲线又会变陡峭且更窄。

Think of this animation in terms of the g-h filter. At each step the g-h filter makes a prediction, takes a measurement, computes the residual (the difference between the prediction and the measurement), and then selects a point on the residual line based on the scaling factor gg. The Kalman filter is doing exactly the same thing, except that the scaling factor gg varies with time. As the filter becomes more confident in its state the scaling factor favors the filter's prediction over the measurement.
可以将这个动画理解为基于 g-h 滤波器的处理过程。在每个步骤中，g-h 滤波器会先进行预测，然后进行测量，接着计算出预测值与实际测量值之间的差异。之后，根据某个缩放因子来选择残差曲线上的一个点。卡尔曼滤波器其实做的是完全相同的事情，只不过缩放因子会随时间而变化。当滤波器对当前状态的置信度越高时，该缩放因子就会更倾向于采用滤波器的预测结果，而非实际测量值。

Example: Extreme Amounts of Noise
示例：极高的噪音水平¶

With the dog filter I didn't put a lot of noise in the signal, and I 'guessed' that the dog was at position 0. How does the filter perform in real world conditions? I will start by injecting more noise in the RFID sensor while leaving the process variance at 2 m22. I will inject an extreme amount of noise - noise that apparently swamps the actual measurement. What does your intuition say about the filter's performance if the sensor has a standard deviation of 300 meters? In other words, an actual position of 1.0 m might be reported as 287.9 m, or -589.6 m, or any other number in roughly that range. Think about it before you scroll down.
在使用了“狗形滤波器”之后，我并没有在信号中加入太多噪声。我推测，那只狗的位置应该为 0。那么，这种滤波器在现实环境中的表现如何呢？我会先在 RFID 传感器中加入更多的噪声，同时保持过程方差为 2 米。我会加入极其大量的噪声——多到几乎淹没真实的测量结果。如果传感器的标准差为 300 米，那么你对这种滤波器的性能有什么直觉上的判断呢？换句话说，实际位置为 1.0 米的数据，可能会被记录为 287.9 米、-589.6 米，或者该范围内的其他数值。请在继续阅读之前先思考一下。

In [28]:  在[28]中：
sensor_var = 300.**2
process_var = 2.
process_model = gaussian(1., process_var)
pos = gaussian(0., 500.)
N = 1000
dog = DogSimulation(pos.mean, 1., sensor_var, process_var)
zs = [dog.move_and_sense() for _ in range(N)]
ps = []

for i in range(N):
    prior = predict(pos, process_model)    
    pos = update(prior, gaussian(zs[i], sensor_var))
    ps.append(pos.mean)

book_plots.plot_measurements(zs, lw=1)
book_plots.plot_filter(ps)
plt.legend(loc=4);

In this example the noise is extreme yet the filter still outputs a nearly straight line! This is an astonishing result! What do you think might be the cause of this performance?
在这个例子中，噪声非常大，但滤波器处理后的输出却几乎呈直线状！这一结果真是令人惊讶！你认为造成这种现象的原因可能是什么？

We get a nearly straight line because our process error is small. A small process error tells the filter that the prediction is very trustworthy, and the prediction is a straight line, so the filter outputs a nearly straight line.
由于我们的工艺误差很小，因此得到的曲线几乎是一条直线。较小的工艺误差意味着预测结果非常可靠，而预测结果本身又呈直线形态，因此滤波器输出的结果也几乎是一条直线。

Example: Incorrect Process Variance
示例：不正确的过程偏差¶

That last filter looks fantastic! Why wouldn't we set the process variance very low, as it guarantees the result will be straight and smooth?
最后一个滤镜的效果真是棒极了！我们当然应该把处理过程中的误差降到最低，这样才能确保最终的结果既平滑又完美。

The process variance tells the filter how much the system is changing over time. If you lie to the filter by setting this number artificially low the filter will not be able to react to changes that are happening. Let's have the dog increase his velocity by a small amount at each time step and see how the filter performs with a process variance of 0.001 m22.
过程方差告诉滤波器：系统随着时间的推移发生了多大的变化。如果你人为地将这个数值设置得过低，从而欺骗了滤波器，那么滤波器就无法对实际发生的变化做出反应。让我们让狗在每个时间步长上都将速度增加一个小数值，然后看看当过程方差为 0.001 米/秒时，滤波器的表现如何。

In [29]:  在[29]中：
sensor_var = 20.
process_var = .001
process_model = gaussian(1., process_var)
pos = gaussian(0., 500.)
N = 100
dog = DogSimulation(pos.mean, 1, sensor_var, process_var*10000)
zs, ps = [], []
for _ in range(N):
    dog.velocity += 0.04
    zs.append(dog.move_and_sense())

for z in zs:
    prior = predict(pos, process_model)    
    pos = update(prior, gaussian(z, sensor_var))
    ps.append(pos.mean)

book_plots.plot_measurements(zs, lw=1)
book_plots.plot_filter(ps)
plt.legend(loc=4);

It is easy to see that the filter is not correctly responding to the measurements. The measurements clearly indicate that the dog is changing speed but the filter has been told that it's predictions are nearly perfect so it almost entirely ignores them. I encourage you to adjust the amount of movement in the dog vs process variance. We will also be studying this topic much more in the later chapters. The key point is to recognize that math requires that the variances correctly describe your system. The filter does not 'notice' that it is diverging from the measurements and correct itself. It computes the Kalman gain from the variance of the prior and the measurement, and forms the estimate depending on which is more accurate.
显然，该滤波器并没有正确地响应各项测量数据。测量结果清楚地表明狗的速度正在发生变化，但滤波器却认为自己的预测结果已经非常准确了，因此几乎完全忽略了这些测量数据。我建议您调整一下与狗的运动相关的参数，以减少误差。我们将在后面的章节中进一步探讨这个话题。关键在于要明白：数学模型要求各种方差能够准确反映系统的实际情况。该滤波器没有意识到自己与实际测量结果之间存在偏差，也无法自行进行调整。它根据先前的预测值和测量结果来计算卡尔曼增益，然后选择更准确的数值作为最终估计结果。

Example: Bad Initial Estimate
示例：不准确的初步估算¶

Now let's look at the results when we make a bad initial estimate of position. To avoid obscuring the results I'll reduce the sensor variance to 30, but set the initial position to 1000 meters. Can the filter recover from a 1000 meter error?
现在，让我们来看看当我们对位置的初始估计有误时，会产生怎样的结果。为避免影响结果的准确性，我会将传感器的误差范围设定为 30，同时将初始位置设置为 1000 米。那么，滤波器能否纠正 1000 米的误差呢？

In [30]:  在[30]中：
sensor_var = 5.**2
process_var = 2.
pos = gaussian(1000., 500.)
process_model = gaussian(1., process_var)
N = 100
dog = DogSimulation(0, 1, sensor_var, process_var)
zs = [dog.move_and_sense() for _ in range(N)]
ps = []

for z in zs:
    prior = predict(pos, process_model)    
    pos = update(prior, gaussian(z, sensor_var))
    ps.append(pos.mean)

book_plots.plot_measurements(zs, lw=1)
book_plots.plot_filter(ps)
plt.legend(loc=4);

Again the answer is yes! Because we are relatively sure about our belief in the sensor (σ2=52σ2=52) after only the first step we have changed our position estimate from 1000 m to roughly 50 m. After another 5-10 measurements we have converged to the correct value. This is how we get around the chicken and egg problem of initial guesses. In practice we would likely assign the first measurement from the sensor as the initial value, but you can see it doesn't matter much if we wildly guess at the initial conditions - the Kalman filter still converges so long as the filter variances are chosen to match the actual process and measurement variances.
答案依然是肯定的！因为，仅仅在第一步之后，我们对传感器的测量结果就相当有信心了：原本估计的距离为 1000 米，之后则降至了大约 50 米。再经过 5 到 10 次测量后，我们就能得到准确的数值。这样一来，我们就解决了初始值选取的问题。实际上，我们通常会直接将传感器的第一次测量结果作为初始值。不过，即便我们最初给出了完全错误的估计，也没关系——只要卡尔曼滤波器的方差设置得当，以匹配实际的过程和测量误差，滤波器最终仍能得到正确的结果。

Example: Large Noise and Bad Initial Estimate
示例：巨大的噪声以及不准确的初始估计值¶

What about the worst of both worlds, large noise and a bad initial estimate?
那如果同时出现最糟糕的情况呢：既有巨大的噪音，又有不准确的初始估计值？

In [31]:  在[31]中：
sensor_var = 30000.
process_var = 2.
pos = gaussian(1000., 500.)
process_model = gaussian(1., process_var)

N = 1000
dog = DogSimulation(0, 1, sensor_var, process_var)
zs = [dog.move_and_sense() for _ in range(N)]
ps = []

for z in zs:
    prior = predict(pos, process_model) 
    pos = update(prior, gaussian(z, sensor_var))
    ps.append(pos.mean)

book_plots.plot_measurements(zs, lw=1)
book_plots.plot_filter(ps)
plt.legend(loc=4);

This time the filter struggles. Notice that the previous example only computed 100 updates, whereas this example uses 1000. By my eye it takes the filter 400 or so iterations to become reasonable accurate, but maybe over 600 before the results are good. Kalman filters are good, but we cannot expect miracles. If we have extremely noisy data and extremely bad initial conditions, this is as good as it gets.
这次，滤波器的处理效果不太理想。请注意，之前的例子中只进行了 100 次更新，而这个例子则进行了 1000 次更新。在我看来，滤波器需要大约 400 次迭代才能达到不错的准确度；不过，要想获得理想的结果，可能需要超过 600 次迭代。卡尔曼滤波器确实不错，但我们不能指望它有奇迹般的效果。如果数据中的噪声非常大，或者初始条件极其糟糕，那这就是目前所能达到的最佳效果了。

Finally, let's implement the suggestion of using the first measurement as the initial position.
最后，让我们按照建议，将第一次测量得到的数值作为初始位置吧。

In [32]:  在[32]中：
sensor_var = 30000.
process_var = 2.
process_model = gaussian(1., process_var)
N = 1000
dog = DogSimulation(0, 1, sensor_var, process_var)
zs = [dog.move_and_sense() for _ in range(N)]

pos = gaussian(zs[0], 500.)
ps = []
for z in zs:
    prior = predict(pos, process_model) 
    pos = update(prior, gaussian(z, sensor_var))
    ps.append(pos.mean)

book_plots.plot_measurements(zs, lw=1)
book_plots.plot_filter(ps)
plt.legend(loc='best');

This simple change significantly improves the results. On some runs it takes 200 iterations or so to settle to a good solution, but other runs it converges very rapidly. This all depends on the amount of noise in the first measurement. A large amount of noise causes the initial estimate to be far from the dog's position.
这一简单的改动显著提升了算法的运行效果。在某些情况下，需要大约 200 次迭代才能得到满意的解决方案；而在其他情况下，算法则能很快收敛到正确结果。这一切都取决于第一次测量时存在的噪声程度。如果噪声较大，那么初始估计值就会与狗的实际位置相差甚远。

200 iterations may seem like a lot, but the amount of noise we are injecting is truly huge. In the real world we use sensors like thermometers, laser range finders, GPS satellites, computer vision, and so on. None have the enormous errors in these examples. A reasonable variance for a cheap thermometer might be 0.2 C∘2∘2, and our code is using 30,000 C∘2∘2.
200 次迭代看起来似乎很多，但实际上，我们所引入的误差幅度实在巨大。在现实世界中，我们使用的传感器有温度计、激光测距仪、GPS 卫星、计算机视觉系统等等。这些传感器的误差远远没有这么大。对于一款便宜的温度计来说，其合理的误差范围应该是 0.2 摄氏度左右，而我们的代码却导致了 30,000 摄氏度的巨大误差。

Exercise: Interactive Plots
练习：交互式图表¶

Implement the Kalman filter using Jupyter Notebook's animation features to allow you to modify the various constants in real time using sliders. Refer to the section Interactive Gaussians in the Gaussians chapter to see how to do this. You will use the interact() function to call a calculation and plotting function. Each parameter passed into interact() automatically gets a slider created for it. I have written the boilerplate for this; you fill in the required code.
利用 Jupyter Notebook 的动画功能来实现卡尔曼滤波器。这样，你就可以通过滑块实时调整各种参数值。具体操作方法请参考“高斯分布”章节中的“交互式高斯函数”部分。你需要使用 interact() 函数来调用相关的计算和绘图功能。传递给 interact() 的每个参数都会自动对应一个滑块。我已经编写好了相关的模板代码，你需要自行填写所需的代码即可。

In [33]:  在[33]中：
from ipywidgets import interact
from kf_book.book_plots import FloatSlider

def plot_kalman_filter(start_pos, 
                       sensor_noise, 
                       velocity, 
                       process_noise):
    plt.figure();
    # your code goes here

interact(plot_kalman_filter,
         start_pos=(-10, 10), 
         sensor_noise=FloatSlider(value=5, min=0, max=100), 
         velocity=FloatSlider(value=1, min=-2., max=2.), 
         process_noise=FloatSlider(value=5, min=0, max=100.));

interactive(children=(IntSlider(value=0, description='start_pos', max=10, min=-10), FloatSlider(value=5.0, con…

Solution  解决方案¶

One possible solution follows. We have sliders for the start position, the amount of noise in the sensor, the amount we move in each time step, and how much movement error there is. Process noise is perhaps the least clear - it models how much the dog wanders off course at each time step, so we add that into the dog's position at each step. I set the random number generator seed so that each redraw uses the same random numbers, allowing us to compare the graphs as we move the sliders.
以下是一种可能的解决方案。我们可以使用滑块来调整起始位置、传感器中的噪声水平、每个时间步长内的移动量以及移动过程中的误差大小。其中， “处理噪声”这个参数比较难以理解——它实际上描述了狗在每个时间步长内偏离预定路径的程度。因此，我们需要将这一数值加到狗在每个时间步长后的位置上。我设置了随机数生成器的种子，这样每次重新计算时都会使用相同的随机数，从而便于我们比较在不同滑块设置下得到的结果。

In [34]:  在[34]中：
from numpy.random import seed 
from ipywidgets import interact

def plot_kalman_filter(start_pos, 
                       sensor_noise, 
                       velocity,
                       process_noise):
    N = 20
    zs, ps = [], []   
    seed(303)
    dog = DogSimulation(start_pos, velocity, sensor_noise, process_noise)
    zs = [dog.move_and_sense() for _ in range(N)]
    pos = gaussian(0., 1000.) # mean and variance
    process_model = gaussian(velocity, process_noise)
    
    for z in zs:    
        pos = predict(pos, process_model)
        pos = update(pos, gaussian(z, sensor_noise))
        ps.append(pos.mean)

    plt.figure()
    plt.plot(zs, c='k', marker='o', linestyle='', label='measurement')
    plt.plot(ps, c='#004080', alpha=0.7, label='filter')
    plt.legend(loc=4);
    plt.show()

interact(plot_kalman_filter,
         start_pos=(-10, 10), 
         sensor_noise=FloatSlider(value=5, min=0., max=100), 
         velocity=FloatSlider(value=1, min=-2., max=2.), 
         process_noise=FloatSlider(value=.1, min=0, max=40));

interactive(children=(IntSlider(value=0, description='start_pos', max=10, min=-10), FloatSlider(value=5.0, con…

Exercise - Nonlinear Systems
练习——非线性系统¶

Our equations for the Kalman filter are linear:
卡尔曼滤波器的方程都是线性的：

N(μ¯,σ¯2)N(μ,σ2)=N(μ,σ2)+N(μmove,σ2move)=N(μ¯,σ¯2)×N(μz,σ2z)N(μ¯,σ¯2)=N(μ,σ2)+N(μmove,σmove2)N(μ,σ2)=N(μ¯,σ¯2)×N(μz,σz2)

Do you suppose that this filter works well or poorly with nonlinear systems?
你认为，这种滤波器在处理非线性系统时，效果如何呢？效果好还是差呢？

Implement a Kalman filter that uses the following equation to generate the measurement value
实现一个卡尔曼滤波器，该滤波器使用以下公式来生成测量值。

for i in range(100):
    z = math.sin(i/3.) * 2

Adjust the variance and initial positions to see the effect. What is, for example, the result of a very bad initial guess?
调整方差和初始值，看看会产生什么效果。比如，如果初始猜测非常糟糕，会得到怎样的结果呢？

In [35]:  在[35]中：
#enter your code here.

Solution  解决方案¶

In [36]:  在[36]中：
import math

sensor_var = 30.
process_var = 2.
pos = gaussian(100., 500.)
process_model = gaussian(1., process_var)

zs, ps = [], []

for i in range(100):
    pos = predict(pos, process_model)

    z = math.sin(i/3.)*2 + randn()*1.2
    zs.append(z)
    
    pos = update(pos, gaussian(z, sensor_var))
    ps.append(pos.mean)

plt.plot(zs, c='r', linestyle='dashed', label='measurement')
plt.plot(ps, c='#004080', label='filter')
plt.legend(loc='best');

Discussion  讨论¶

This is terrible! The output is not at all like a sin wave, except in the grossest way. With linear systems we could add extreme amounts of noise to our signal and still extract a very accurate result, but here even modest noise creates a very bad result.
这太糟糕了！输出信号根本不像是正弦波，其形态与正弦波相差甚远。在线性系统中，我们可以在信号中加入大量噪声，但仍然能得到相当准确的结果。而在这里，哪怕是少量的噪声也会导致极其糟糕的结果。

If we recall the g-h Filter chapter we can understand what is happening here. The structure of the g-h filter requires that the filter output chooses a value part way between the prediction and measurement. A varying signal like this one is always accelerating, whereas our process model assumes constant velocity, so the filter is mathematically guaranteed to always lag the input signal.
如果我们回顾一下 g-h 滤波器的相关内容，就能理解这里发生的情况。g-h 滤波器的结构决定了：其输出值必然介于预测值和测量值之间。像这样的变化信号始终处于加速状态，而我们的过程模型则假设信号始终以恒定速度运动。因此，从数学上来说，滤波器的输出值必然总是滞后于输入信号。

Very shortly after practitioners began implementing Kalman filters they recognized the poor performance of them for nonlinear systems and began devising ways of dealing with it. Later chapters are devoted to this problem.
在从业者开始使用卡尔曼滤波器后不久，他们就发现该滤波器在处理非线性系统时效果不佳。于是，他们开始寻求解决这一问题的方法。后面的章节将专门探讨这个问题。

Fixed Gain Filters  固定增益滤波器¶

Embedded computers usually have extremely limited processors. Many do not have floating point circuitry. These simple equations can impose a heavy burden on the chip. This is less true as technology advances, but do not underestimate the value of spending one dollar less on a processor when you will be buying millions of them.
嵌入式计算机的处理器性能通常极为有限。许多嵌入式计算机甚至没有浮点运算功能。这些简单的计算公式可能会给芯片带来很大的负担。不过，随着技术的进步，这种情况有所改善。不过，千万不要低估了这样一个事实：当需要购买数百万个处理器时，每个处理器多节省一美元的成本也是相当可观的。

In the example above the variance of the filter converged to a fixed value. This will always happen if the variance of the measurement and process is a constant. You can take advantage of this fact by running simulations to determine what the variance converges to. Then you can hard code this value into your filter. So long as you initialize the filter to a good starting guess (I recommend using the first measurement as your initial value) the filter will perform very well. For example, the dog tracking filter can be reduced to this:
在上面的例子中，滤波器的方差最终会收敛到一个固定值。只要测量值和过程中的方差保持恒定，这种情况必然会发生。你可以利用这一特性，通过模拟来确定方差最终会收敛到什么数值。然后，你可以将该数值直接写入滤波器中。只要给滤波器一个合理的初始值（我建议使用第一次测量得到的数值作为初始值），滤波器就能发挥出很好的性能。例如，狗的跟踪滤波器就可以简化为这样的形式：

def update(x, z):
    K = .13232  # experimentally derived Kalman gain
    y = z - x   # residual
    x = x + K*y # posterior
    return x
    
def predict(x):
    return x + vel*dt
